title: Guard Clauses for Cleaner Logic
summary: Use early checks to simplify nested conditionals.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to Guard Clauses
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Guard clauses are a programming pattern that allows you to simplify your code by handling edge cases early, thus avoiding deep nesting of conditionals.

sketchBlocks.title: Benefits of Guard Clauses
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Using guard clauses can make your code more readable and maintainable. It helps in clearly defining the conditions that lead to early exits from a function.

quizDraft.title: Implement a Guard Clause
quizDraft.prompt: Write a function that checks if a number is positive. If it is not, return 'Not positive'. Otherwise, return 'Positive'.
quizDraft.hint: Think about how to handle the negative case first.

help.concept: Guard clauses allow you to exit a function early if certain conditions are met.
help.hint_1: Check if the number is less than or equal to zero.
help.hint_2: Use a return statement to exit the function if the condition is true.

quizDraft.title: Validate User Input
quizDraft.prompt: Create a function that validates user input for age. If the age is less than 0 or greater than 120, return 'Invalid age'. Otherwise, return 'Valid age'.
quizDraft.hint: Consider the boundaries for valid age.

help.concept: Guard clauses can help you validate input by checking for invalid cases first.
help.hint_1: Check if the age is outside the valid range.
help.hint_2: Use an if statement to return 'Invalid age' for out-of-range values.

quizDraft.title: Understanding Guard Clauses
quizDraft.prompt: What is the main purpose of using guard clauses in programming?
quizDraft.hint: They help simplify the logic in your functions.

help.concept: Guard clauses are used to handle edge cases early in a function.
help.hint_1: They reduce the need for nested conditionals.

quizDraft.title: Benefits of Guard Clauses
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are benefits of using guard clauses?
quizDraft.hint: Think about code clarity and maintenance.

help.concept: Guard clauses improve code clarity and reduce complexity.
help.hint_1: They help in avoiding deep nesting.

quizDraft.title: Complete the Guard Clause
quizDraft.prompt: A guard clause is used to handle __________ early in a function.
quizDraft.template: A guard clause is used to handle {blank} early in a function.
quizDraft.hint: Think about what conditions might cause a function to exit early.
